summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring extensive employee recognition presentations for police, fire, and service department personnel. The board approved a resolution to collect 5% cable franchise fees from Alt Fiber in addition to existing Spectrum fees, increased general fund revenue by $463,623 from host community dispensary funds, approved the purchase of dais tablets, scheduled a zoning workshop, and expressed support for a Clermont County land use plan grant application.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Feb 24 - Mar 10, 2026) passed
Motion to recess for approximately 15 minutes for employee recognition refreshments passed
Motion to schedule Board of Trustees special meeting/zoning workshop for Tuesday March 31 at 6 PM passed
Motion to approve purchase of eight tablets with stands for the dais at $341.75 each passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-011-2026 to collect 5% cable franchise fees including advertising revenue from video service providers passed
Motion to increase general fund revenue (host community dispensary fund) by $463,623 passed
Motion to approve letter of support for Clermont County countywide land use plan grant application passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:18 PM passed
action_items: Evan Corday to send franchise fee resolution to Alt Fiber and resend to Spectrum
Evan Corday to perform annual audits of cable franchise fee collections from Spectrum and Alt Fiber
Fiscal Officer Laura to roll dispensary fund money into the savings/money market account
Administrator Rose and Donna Rosick to finalize OPWC grant details for road paving (Smith Fay and Charles Snyder roads)
Board to sign and send letter of support for Clermont County land use plan grant to fiscal officer
Schedule and prepare for zoning workshop special meeting on March 31, inviting Zoning Commission, BZA, and Mr. Mayoff
key_amounts: $135,000 local match for road paving project
$463,623 increase in general fund from host community dispensary fund
$283,000 in dispensary back pay and first two 2026 payments received
~$18,000 per month average dispensary payments going forward
$94,000 collected from Spectrum cable franchise fees in 2025
$132,787 collected from Spectrum in 2020 (peak year shown)
$10,000 per month earned in interest on money market savings account
5% cable franchise fee rate on gross cable TV revenues
3.6% of 10% excise tax on adult-use marijuana sales goes to host community
notable_topics: Employee recognition presentations for service department (winter snow operations), police, and fire personnel
2025 Officer of the Year award to Officer Wesley Lyons
Life-Saving Award for cardiac arrest response on January 3, 2026
Drug interdiction awards for Sergeant Collier and Officer McAllister
Meritorious service commendation for Detective Sergeant McMillan (homicide, child abuse, financial crimes investigations)
Traffic crash reconstructionist recognition for Officer Tyler Smith
Cable franchise fee resolution to collect from Alt Fiber in addition to Spectrum
Host community dispensary fund revenue increase of $463,623
OPWC road paving grant for Smith Fay and Charles Snyder roads (tentative approval)
USPS approval to recognize Goshen as preferred mailing city name for all township addresses
New fire station construction nearing completion
Clermont County land use plan grant support letter
Purchase of dais tablets for board members
Discussion of investing dispensary revenue in money market savings account
attendees: Chief Myers (absent - at National Fallen Firefighters Memorial)
notes: Trustee Allen was absent and sent regrets. Chief Myers was absent attending National Fallen Firefighters Memorial in Baltimore. The meeting included a 15-minute recess for refreshments provided by the Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association following employee recognition. The board discussed putting dispensary revenue into a money market savings account earning approximately $10,000/month interest, with the understanding that dispensary funds were originally intended to support emergency services (fire and police). The OPWC grant notification was described as 95% certain but not yet officially confirmed by OPWC. The cable franchise fee presentation noted a Maple Heights Ohio Supreme Court case that prevented municipalities from collecting franchise fees from streaming services like Netflix.
title: BOT 3-10-26
